fault_hypothesis: The `overlaps` function at line 64 incorrectly uses non-strict inequalities (`<=`, `>=`) to check for non-overlapping intervals. This fails to detect when two intervals are adjacent, which should be treated as an overlap.

spec.expr: not (b[1] == a[0] or b[0] == a[1]) or __cbfl_result

constraints.intent: The overlaps function should return True if intervals are adjacent (touching boundaries).
If the loop continues to the next iteration, the candidate must not overlap or be adjacent to the current slot.

spec.expr: all(candidate[1] < s[0] or candidate[0] > s[1] for s in existing)

constraints.intent: If the candidate is merged into the schedule, it must not overlap or be adjacent to any of the original slots.

spec.expr: not __cbfl_result[0] or all(candidate[1] < s[0] or candidate[0] > s[1] for s in existing)

constraints.intent: If the function accepts the candidate, then the candidate must not have overlapped (including adjacency) any existing slot.
